primary_reason: This rheumatoid tenosynovitis case uses MRI for a noninfectious diagnosis and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This population survey combines active household and passive facility case finding to reduce underestimation of infected wounds and neglected tropical skin diseases.
This scoping review compares constructed and established area-level socioeconomic indicators, including their limitations for analyzing infectious and parasitic mortality inequalities.
This integrative review organizes the ascertainment, detection, diagnosis, classification, reporting, and notification mechanisms that can underestimate mpox surveillance.
This review describes cerebrovascular phenotypes among people with HIV but does not develop or compare a reusable epidemiologic measurement or modeling method.
This study validates a modified disc-diffusion test and lateral-flow assay against genotyped isolates for detecting otherwise missed ESBL and AmpC resistance mechanisms.
This study proposes a two-tier Bayesian hierarchical model with model averaging to estimate spatially and temporally varying COVID-19 incidence and detection rates.
This clinical overview summarizes tick-borne encephalitis epidemiology, presentation, and diagnosis without evaluating a transferable surveillance or analytic method.
This study evaluates a resource-appropriate phenotypic identification battery against automated identification for detecting underrecognized Streptococcus anginosus group infections.
This retrospective case-finding study tests stored samples from unexplained viral CNS infections to measure missed tick-borne encephalitis diagnoses and sampling determinants.
This cross-sectional survey reports occupational blood and body-fluid exposures and associated factors using ordinary regression without a distinct transferable method contribution.
This pertussis-vaccination evaluation compares two decision-tree and Markov structures and uses sensitivity and value-of-information analyses to characterize structural uncertainty.
This randomized framing experiment evaluates how safety and recipient-focused messages affect blood-donation intentions and perceived causes of underreported sexual behavior.
This review compares foodborne-disease outbreak patterns by food-preparation setting across countries and identifies how heterogeneous surveillance and underreporting limit inference.
This study applies burden multipliers and economic valuation to translate a decline in reported Salmonella cases into illnesses, hospitalizations, deaths, and costs averted.
This single-isolate case report characterizes a rare yeast infection but does not evaluate a general diagnostic or surveillance method.
This living systematic review applies risk-of-bias, GRADE, and equity-focused subgroup analyses to continually synthesize corticosteroid evidence for COVID-19.
This outbreak investigation combines field case collection, PCR, gross pathology, and histopathology to confirm underrecognized Marek's disease in vaccinated poultry.
This study audits immunization counts across registers, facility reports, and DHIS2 and diagnoses organizational causes of under- and over-reporting.
This case study offers a qualitative interpretation of changing dengue reports during COVID-19 without a clearly specified reusable analytic or correction design.
This clinical review summarizes a rare postinfectious neurologic syndrome and its diagnosis and treatment without contributing an epidemiologic method.
This global analysis uses uncertainty ranges and contour plots to rank COVID-19 mortality against familiar causes despite uncertain fatality and underreporting parameters.
This mechanistic COVID-19 model simulates interacting variants, multidose vaccination, waning immunity, behavior, and underreporting across five countries and policy scenarios.
This branching-process study develops and validates on simulated and real outbreaks a method for jointly estimating real-time transmission heterogeneity and reproduction numbers.
This Monte Carlo quantitative microbial risk assessment estimates pathogen-specific waterborne illness and simulates partial and complete private-well treatment failures.

This systematic review supplements incomplete surveillance by reconstructing healthcare-associated hepatitis transmission events, settings, routes, and infection-control failures from multiple sources.
The title indicates possible COVID-19 underreporting among psoriasis patients, but the abstract-free letter provides insufficient content to judge its method contribution.
This nationally sampled mixed-mode survey models suspected and confirmed COVID-19 infections to estimate the degree of undertesting and case underreporting in Pakistan.
This Bayesian time-series SIR model estimates influenza transmissibility and separates behavioral effects after accounting for changing underreporting and population immunity.
This hospital point-prevalence study reconstructs the diarrhea-to-specimen-to-test cascade to identify where Clostridioides difficile infections are missed.
This global counterfactual model simulates COVID-19 cases, deaths, and economic costs with versus without vaccination while adjusting for underreported outcomes.
This seroepidemiologic study converts pertussis-antibody thresholds and an assumed infection window into incidence estimates for comparison with notification data.
This qualitative study diagnoses structural and behavioral causes of vaccine adverse-event underreporting across multiple levels of Ghana's safety system.
This implementation study uses preimplementation and postimplementation audits to correct gaps in COVID-19 vaccine adverse-event reporting practices and training.
This mini-review discusses possible biological relationships among gastrointestinal infections, cancer, and inflammatory bowel disease without evaluating a reusable epidemiologic method.
This general monkeypox review summarizes transmission, symptoms, and prevention but does not compare or develop surveillance or analytic methods.
This study integrates citizen-science bird observations through a two-stage framework to map species-associated avian-influenza surveillance priorities.
This compartmental Zika model jointly represents mosquito and sexual transmission, estimates underreporting from Colombian incidence, and simulates targeted control scenarios.
This scoping review systematically classifies Giardia outbreak transmission routes and identifies molecular-typing limitations that impede attribution of zoonotic transmission.
This age-structured SEIRS model integrates contact matrices, setting-specific cases, and age- and period-specific underdetection to estimate transmission contributions.
This seminar review describes cardiovascular effects of infections and data gaps in low-resource settings without presenting a transferable study method.
This study uses all-payer billing data and a new ICD code to characterize a rare post-SARS-CoV-2 syndrome beyond voluntary surveillance reports.
This international cohort and meta-analysis compare telemedicine with in-person wound review and demonstrate differential under-ascertainment of surgical-site infection.
This systematic review synthesizes asymptomatic Leishmania incidence across settings and diagnostic techniques to expose a hidden reservoir of infection.
This review concerns noninfectious vaping-associated lung injury and supplies no concrete route for transferring its case synthesis to infectious-disease methods.
This travel-health review summarizes chikungunya risks, presentation, and prevention without substantively evaluating a reusable epidemiologic method.
This ecological study combines time-series and spatial hotspot analyses to locate and track childhood tuberculosis clusters across Brazil.
This assessment triangulates literature, official reports, and a member-state survey to evaluate wild-suidae contributions to African swine fever surveillance.
This pertussis study combines spatial autocorrelation and space-time scan statistics to identify persistent geographic and seasonal clusters.
This study contextualizes pediatric COVID-19 mortality by ranking it against standardized competing causes across age groups and rolling 12-month periods.
This interrupted time-series study evaluates suicide during the pandemic but states no concrete transfer of its noninfectious outcome analysis to infectious-disease methods.
This qualitative anatomy study concerns sexual pleasure and pain and provides no infectious-disease outcome or concrete methodological transfer route.
This infectious-disease model integrates surveillance incidence, vaccination uptake, and under-ascertainment assumptions to estimate age- and state-specific immunologic susceptibility.
This preprint reports the same national wastewater time-series analysis, authors, study period, and principal metrics as the subsequently published retained record.
This study experimentally estimates recovery, sewer partitioning, and two-phase decay parameters and evaluates their influence on wastewater back-estimation of Campylobacter.
This multinational vaccine-intent survey combines behavioral segmentation, random forests, and explicit adjustment for response-reporting bias.
This systematic review and meta-analysis combines observational studies and case reports to assess the detected and likely hidden burden of histoplasmosis.
This review describes HTLV-1 prevalence, diseases, geography, and genetic diversity without developing or substantively comparing a transferable method.
This systematic review maps infections and immune-deficiency indicators across hematologic-cancer treatment classes while identifying systematically missing laboratory outcomes.
This validation study compares recalled maternal infections with medical records using sensitivity, specificity, agreement, and changes in estimated associations.
This incidence study concerns carcinoma arising from pilonidal disease and provides no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This systematic review concerns cancer metastases to the hand and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This survey diagnoses knowledge, identification-skill, workflow, and feedback deficiencies underlying delayed or missed reporting of infectious dermatologic diseases.
This qualitative follow-up uses interviews and focus groups to explain quantitative declines in malaria care, diagnosis, prevention, and reporting during COVID-19.
This antiviral review catalogs candidate compounds and viral targets for Mayaro virus without comparing epidemiologic or surveillance methods.
This scoping review systematically identifies behavioral, service, surveillance, cultural, and humanitarian mechanisms that obscure HIV trends among people who inject drugs.
This study quantifies pertussis underreporting by converting antibody seroprevalence into infection rates and comparing them with age-specific notifications.
This study samples multiple contiguous catheter-system segments and analyzes organism concordance to characterize contamination prevalence and possible transmission direction.
This study develops and compares two automated register-based algorithms against mandatory notifications for detecting hospital SARS-CoV-2 clusters.
This computational epidemiologic framework embeds flexible time-varying testing and infection splines and jointly fits case and seroprevalence data using Bayesian inference.
This paired longitudinal study applies untargeted lipidomics and pathway analysis to identify persistent molecular signatures two years after SARS-CoV-2 infection.
This meta-analysis pools excess-mortality and transmission-model estimates to derive an underreporting-adjusted national SARS-CoV-2 infection-fatality rate.
This simulation-based cost-of-illness analysis extrapolates surgical-site infections and their direct, productivity, and premature-mortality costs across Australian jurisdictions.
This study develops an ARCH-based Bayesian synthetic-likelihood correction for misreported nonstationary series and evaluates it through simulation before applying it to COVID-19.
This study links population-survey responses to clinic records and quantifies the sensitivity and specificity of self-reported HIV testing, status, and treatment.
This serosurvey measures SARS-CoV-2 antibodies in a surveillance-missed population and contrasts infection prevalence with reported circulation in Togo.
This systematic review compares outbreak-investigation components for carbapenem-resistant Pseudomonas and assesses the evidence supporting environmental, patient, and staff screening.
This study repurposes serial antenatal specimens for national serosurveillance and compares adjusted seroprevalence with PCR-based reporting across pandemic periods.
This routine surveillance summary describes yersiniosis counts, incidence, hospitalization, and age patterns without a distinct reusable analytic contribution.
This scoping review evaluates China's multisectoral rabies-elimination progress and prescribes coordinated vaccination, population management, surveillance, prophylaxis, and education strategies.
This repeated facility survey links availability of gonorrhea testing methods with hospital reporting patterns to diagnose a source of surveillance undercounting.
This cross-sectional survey describes sharps injuries and reporting behavior but contributes no distinct infectious-surveillance measurement or analytic method.
This integrative review organizes and weighs ten competing explanations, including underreporting, for Africa's comparatively low recorded COVID-19 burden.
These two bacteremia case reports raise awareness of an overlooked pathogen but do not evaluate a generalizable identification or surveillance method.
This large EHR study compares code-based and symptom-based definitions of long COVID and applies the same measurement framework to post-influenza illness.
This case-control study reports scrub-typhus exposure associations using standard regression without a distinct transferable design or measurement contribution.
This study combines outbreak data, underdiagnosis multipliers, severity distributions, and economic valuation to estimate flour-associated foodborne-disease burden.
This systematic review synthesizes how international health emergencies disrupt infectious-disease notification, diagnosis, treatment, vaccination, and surveillance systems.
These two Erysipelothrix bacteremia cases question an assumed clinical association but do not test a transferable epidemiologic or diagnostic method.
This population-oriented serosurvey compares measured SARS-CoV-2 exposure with prior diagnoses to assess infections missed by Somalia's surveillance system.
This clinical update focuses on smoking, alcohol, and diabetes and provides no concrete infectious-disease transfer route for its discussion of reporting bias.
This repeated blood-donor serosurveillance study adjusts for assay and sampling properties and estimates changing SARS-CoV-2 underreporting across pandemic waves.
This case report and literature synthesis describe the clinical distribution and outcomes of oral cryptococcosis without evaluating a transferable method.
This study formulates stochastic-frontier epidemic models that estimate reported and undocumented infections with geographically varying reporting rates from case data alone.
This cohort describes pediatric tuberculosis notifications and severity before and during COVID-19 without a distinct correction, counterfactual, or measurement method.
This study validates self-reported smoking against plasma cotinine and quantifies concordance, sensitivity, and specificity across HIV and pregnancy strata.
This invited review summarizes bovine abortion incidence, risk factors, and causes but does not substantively compare surveillance or diagnostic methods.
This stochastic reporting-chain model estimates stage-specific COVID-19 ascertainment probabilities under changing laboratory-testing criteria in Toronto.
This pre-pandemic versus pandemic comparison describes reported chlamydia trends without a formal counterfactual or reusable correction method.
This study demonstrates dengue genotyping from archived paraffin-embedded fatal-case tissues, enabling retrospective molecular surveillance when fresh specimens are unavailable.
This livestock study compares culture with LipL32 qPCR and sequencing and shows how molecular testing detects pathogenic Leptospira missed by the reference panel.
This study probabilistically links three HIV databases and applies log-linear capture-recapture models to estimate undiagnosed cases and source-specific underreporting.
This scoping review audits consent, protocol registration, ethics, anonymization, data sharing, and FAIR reporting as reproducibility and study-conduct indicators.
This review addresses underreported food intake in obesity research and supplies no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This mechanistic state-space study modifies the unscented Kalman filter for nonnegative epidemic states and estimates behavioral, infectiousness, and reporting processes.
This study triangulates burial reports, cemetery imagery, social-media surveys, and representative serology within a model for settings lacking complete death registration.
This genotype-to-phenotype analysis concerns inherited kidney disease and provides no concrete transfer route to infectious-disease methods.
This postmarketing study reports infection and malignancy incidence across heterogeneous rheumatoid-arthritis registries without evaluating a distinct surveillance method.
This survey estimates vaccine adverse-event reporting fractions and identifies patient, event-severity, and vaccine predictors of spontaneous-reporting failure.
This HIV-care study uses mediation analysis to connect stigma and depression with nondisclosure of alcohol use, identifying a mechanism of exposure-measurement error.
This study develops Bayesian inference that integrates serial serosurveys, registered cases, and antibody-development data to estimate time-varying infection underreporting.
This compartmental model integrates wastewater genome copies with clinical cases to estimate infections, reporting gaps, delays, and fecal shedding.
This HIV and hepatitis C study evaluates agreement among ethyl glucuronide, breath alcohol, and self-reported drinking as alternative exposure measurements.
This head-to-head study estimates the diagnostic performance of an aerobic Campylobacter medium against standard microaerophilic culture in clinical stool samples.
This paper identifies gender-identity underreporting as a generalizability defect and recommends inclusive measurement in COVID-19 clinical trials.
This personal historical account recounts tick-borne encephalitis vaccine development but does not present sufficient evaluative method content.
This registry audit quantifies nonreporting and premature discontinuation of sepsis trials and models trial characteristics associated with each reproducibility failure.
This systematic review audits epidemic-vaccine protocols and publications for pregnancy eligibility and pregnancy-outcome reporting, documenting a consequential evidence gap.
This longitudinal population cohort repeatedly measures infection- and vaccine-derived antibodies to estimate changing seroprevalence, underreporting, and infection risk factors.
This review addresses noninfectious bowel disease diagnosis and treatment, and infectious mimicry alone does not establish a concrete epidemiologic transfer route.
This study tracks sequential changes in case-to-wastewater and hospitalization-to-wastewater ratios to diagnose declining clinical ascertainment across disadvantaged communities.
This convenience survey describes mpox knowledge, stigma, vaccination willingness, and self-reported diagnoses without a distinct transferable measurement contribution.
This review compares pediatric Clostridioides difficile diagnostic and treatment practice in Saudi Arabia with international recommendations and identifies underreporting risks.
This national time-series study compares two wastewater metrics and validates thresholds for detecting high COVID-19 case and hospitalization rates.
This methodological perspective explains how within-host and between-host dynamic models should be integrated with wastewater measurements for prevalence estimation and forecasting.
This study compares Bayesian epidemic-forecasting models through simulated weekly forecasting, explicitly representing underreported cases and delayed retrospective data additions.
This review synthesizes needlestick causes, prevention, and management while identifying inconsistent guidance and systematic underreporting among dental workers.
This prospective cohort uses scheduled serology, weekly active surveillance, and unscheduled illness visits to measure dengue beyond passive notifications.
This One Health review synthesizes animal toxoplasmosis surveillance and recommends risk-based farm, abattoir, meat-safety, and consumer-control measures.
This study validates alternative EHR case definitions for chronic hepatitis C against chart review using sensitivity, specificity, and predictive values.
This study develops a clustered Bayesian hierarchical Poisson approach to estimate regional syphilis reporting probabilities and correct incidence rates.
This multicountry study applies wastewater ddPCR to community surveillance of tuberculosis drug-resistance genes where clinical access and reporting are limited.
This study develops a knowledge-rule and medical-record decision-support system and evaluates its effect on missed infectious-disease reports using interrupted time series.
This scoping review compares the functionality, implementation, and testing of digital tools for postdischarge surgical-site infection surveillance.
This survey describes sharps injuries and reporting among dermatologic surgeons without developing a distinct infectious-risk measurement or correction method.
This qualitative implementation analysis converts routine user feedback into functional changes for a confidential mail-order sexually transmitted infection testing service.
This compartmental model fits early COVID-19 transmission in 54 African countries to estimate reproduction numbers, symptom severity, and ascertainment rates.
This national linkage study compares diagnosis-coded respiratory hospitalizations with microbiologically confirmed RSV to quantify underreporting and misclassification.
This study validates self-reported condom use against vaginal Y-chromosome DNA and quantifies discordance and biomarker test performance.
This One Health study combines serology, urine PCR, sequencing, and phylogenetics to assess domestic dogs as Leptospira reservoirs in complex ecosystems.
This randomized trial compares SMS-assisted active vaccine-safety follow-up with passive reporting and measures their relative adverse-event detection rates.
This longitudinal program evaluation measures how free specimen collection, expanded pathogen panels, and eradication initiatives change bovine abortion submissions.
This narrative review translates global and Turkish pertussis evidence into surveillance, diagnostic access, booster-vaccination, and catch-up recommendations.
This study compares reproduction-number estimates derived from reported cases with estimates derived from multiple severe outcomes under fluctuating ascertainment.
This pertussis cost-of-illness framework combines medical, patient, and indirect costs and tests sensitivity to incidence and underreporting assumptions.
This pregnancy cohort supplements self-reported alcohol exposure with neonatal phosphatidylethanol measurements when estimating birth outcomes among women with and without HIV.
This regional initiative report compares meningococcal case definitions, confirmation capacity, and notification practices and identifies surveillance changes needed to prevent misreporting.
This prospective cohort uses repeated nucleocapsid serology to estimate SARS-CoV-2 incidence and changing risk factors across vaccine and variant eras.
This cancer patho-genomic case concerns missed SMARCB1 alterations and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
This study constructs pre-vaccination counterfactual incidence from long national time series to estimate reported vaccine-preventable disease cases averted.
